description: The claude.ai remote-control bridge subsystem. Connects Claude Code to claude.ai via WebSocket/SSE for remote-controlled REPL sessions. Encompasses the full bridge lifecycle: session creation, JWT authentication and token refresh, work-dispatch polling, message ingress/egress, permission callbacks, transport layers (env-based and env-less), capacity management, trusted device handling, and bridge UI/debug tooling. Two core architectures coexist: the env-based path (replBridge.ts/bridgeMain.ts, ~5400 lines combined) using the Environments API work-dispatch layer, and the env-less path (remoteBridgeCore.ts, ~1000 lines) connecting directly to session-ingress without the Environments API.

nodes.name: DaemonBridge
nodes.description: Multi-session persistent bridge daemon (claude remote-control). Orchestrates the full environment lifecycle: registers a bridge environment with the Environments API, polls for incoming work items, spawns child CLI processes per session (in worktree or same-dir mode), manages session timeouts, handles graceful shutdown with SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation, and supports session resume via --session-id. bridgeMain.ts (~3000 lines) is the main orchestration loop with backoff/retry logic; sessionRunner.ts (~550 lines) spawns child processes with --sdk-url flags, parses their stdout for activity/permission events, and manages stdin/kill lifecycle.

edges.description: Registers environments, polls for work, acknowledges/stops work items, sends permission responses, heartbeats, deregisters on shutdown
Gets trusted device tokens for API headers, decodes work secrets, refreshes JWT tokens, validates bridge IDs
Creates the BridgeLogger for terminal status display, updates idle/attached/reconnecting/failed states and session counts
Uses BridgeConfig/SessionHandle/SpawnMode types, poll interval config, capacity wake signals, debug utilities

nodes.name: ReplBridge
nodes.description: REPL-embedded single-session bridge that runs inside the interactive CLI process. Connects the local REPL session to claude.ai for remote control, using the Environments API work-dispatch layer. replBridge.ts (~2400 lines) contains initBridgeCore: environment registration, poll loop, work acknowledgment, WebSocket/SSE transport management, message forwarding (ingress→REPL and REPL→server), echo deduplication, token refresh, and reconnection with backoff. initReplBridge.ts (~570 lines) is the REPL-specific wrapper handling OAuth, feature gates, git context, session title derivation, and env-less vs env-based path selection. replBridgeTransport.ts (~370 lines) abstracts the v1 (WebSocket HybridTransport) and v2 (SSE+CCRClient) transport layers. replBridgeHandle.ts (~36 lines) provides a global pointer to the active bridge handle for tools and slash commands.

edges.description: Registers environment, polls for work, acknowledges work, sends permission responses via the BridgeApiClient
Processes ingress messages via handleIngressMessage, handles server control requests, builds result messages, deduplicates echoes
Decodes work secrets, builds SDK URLs, refreshes JWT tokens, checks bridge enabled gates, gets trusted device tokens
initReplBridge selects and delegates to initEnvLessBridgeCore when the tengu_bridge_repl_v2 gate is enabled
Uses FlushGate for message ordering, CapacityWake for poll sleep, PollIntervalConfig for timing, types for BridgeConfig/SessionHandle

nodes.name: EnvLessBridge
nodes.description: Env-less bridge core that connects directly to session-ingress without the Environments API work-dispatch layer. Gated by tengu_bridge_repl_v2 flag, REPL-only. Uses a simplified lifecycle: POST /v1/code/sessions to create a session, POST /v1/code/sessions/{id}/bridge to get a worker JWT and epoch (replacing register/poll/ack), then SSE+CCRClient transport. Supports proactive JWT refresh, 401 recovery with transport rebuild (preserving SSE sequence numbers), and graceful teardown with session archival. remoteBridgeCore.ts (~1000 lines) is the main core. envLessBridgeConfig.ts (~165 lines) provides GrowthBook-tuned retry/timeout/heartbeat configuration. codeSessionApi.ts (~170 lines) contains thin HTTP wrappers for the CCR v2 code-session API.

edges.description: Reuses the same handleIngressMessage, handleServerControlRequest, and echo dedup machinery as ReplBridge
Uses createTokenRefreshScheduler for JWT lifecycle, getTrustedDeviceToken for elevated auth, buildCCRv2SdkUrl for URL construction
Uses FlushGate for message ordering during initial history flush, types for ReplBridgeHandle/BridgeState

nodes.name: BridgeApi
nodes.description: HTTP API client layer for bridge backend communication. bridgeApi.ts (~540 lines) implements the BridgeApiClient interface: environment registration, work polling with reclaim, work acknowledgment, stop (with retry), heartbeat, deregistration, permission response sending, session archival, and session reconnection. Includes BridgeFatalError for non-retryable errors (auth failures, expired environments), OAuth 401 refresh-and-retry, and trusted device token header injection. createSession.ts (~384 lines) creates sessions on bridge environments via POST /v1/sessions with optional conversation history pre-population and git source metadata. bridgeConfig.ts (~48 lines) centralizes auth token and base URL resolution with ant-only dev overrides.

edges.description: Uses access tokens from bridgeConfig, trusted device tokens for X-Trusted-Device-Token header
Implements BridgeApiClient/PermissionResponseEvent types, uses debugUtils for error description and secret redaction

nodes.name: MessageHandling
nodes.description: Shared message processing layer used by both env-based and env-less bridge cores. bridgeMessaging.ts (~460 lines) provides transport-layer helpers: handleIngressMessage parses incoming WebSocket/SSE data and routes user messages, control responses, and result events; handleServerControlRequest processes server-initiated control requests (permission prompts); makeResultMessage builds completion events; isEligibleBridgeMessage filters messages for forwarding; extractTitleText derives session titles from first user message; BoundedUUIDSet provides ring-buffer-based echo deduplication. inboundMessages.ts (~80 lines) extracts and normalizes content from inbound user messages including image blocks. inboundAttachments.ts (~175 lines) resolves file_uuid attachments by downloading from the OAuth files API and writing to local disk. bridgePermissionCallbacks.ts (~43 lines) defines the BridgePermissionCallbacks interface and validates permission response payloads.

edges.description: Uses ReplBridgeTransport type for transport parameter typing, debugUtils for logging

nodes.name: AuthAndGating
nodes.description: Authentication, security, and feature gating for bridge sessions. jwtUtils.ts (~256 lines) provides JWT decode (payload, expiry), token refresh scheduling with proactive renewal before expiry, and configurable retry. trustedDevice.ts (~210 lines) manages trusted device tokens for elevated-security bridge sessions — enrollment during login, keychain storage via secureStorage, and GrowthBook-gated header injection. workSecret.ts (~127 lines) decodes base64url work secrets from the Environments API, builds WebSocket SDK URLs (v1 and CCR v2), registers workers, and compares session IDs. sessionIdCompat.ts (~57 lines) translates between cse_* (infrastructure) and session_* (compat API) session ID formats for the CCR v2 compat layer. bridgeEnabled.ts (~202 lines) provides runtime and blocking entitlement checks: OAuth subscription validation, GrowthBook feature gates (tengu_ccr_bridge, tengu_bridge_repl_v2), minimum version enforcement, and CSE shim gating.

edges.description: Uses WorkSecret type definition from types.ts, debugUtils for error logging

nodes.name: BridgeDisplay
nodes.description: Terminal UI, status display, and crash-recovery for bridge sessions. bridgeUI.ts (~530 lines) implements the BridgeLogger interface: renders the live terminal display with QR code, shimmer animations, spinner frames, idle/attached/reconnecting/failed states, multi-session bullet lists with per-session activity summaries, and session count indicators. bridgeStatusUtil.ts (~163 lines) provides pure formatting helpers: URL builders for connect/session links, duration formatting, activity abbreviation, OSC-8 hyperlink wrapping, and footer text construction. bridgePointer.ts (~210 lines) manages crash-recovery pointers — persistent files written during active sessions and checked on next startup to offer --session-id resume, with TTL-based staleness detection and git worktree fanout scanning.

edges.description: Uses BridgeConfig, BridgeLogger, SessionActivity, SpawnMode types from types.ts

nodes.name: CorePrimitives
nodes.description: Shared type definitions, configuration, debug infrastructure, and concurrency primitives that all other bridge modules depend on. types.ts (~262 lines) defines the core protocol and dependency-injection types: WorkData/WorkResponse/WorkSecret for the Environments API, BridgeConfig for bridge instance configuration, BridgeApiClient interface, SessionHandle/SessionSpawner for process management, and BridgeLogger for UI abstraction. pollConfig.ts (~110 lines) and pollConfigDefaults.ts (~82 lines) provide GrowthBook-tuned poll interval configuration with safety floors. bridgeDebug.ts (~135 lines) provides ant-only fault injection for testing recovery paths via /bridge-kick. debugUtils.ts (~141 lines) provides secret redaction, error description extraction, HTTP status parsing, and debug truncation. flushGate.ts (~71 lines) implements a state machine for gating message writes during initial history flush. capacityWake.ts (~56 lines) provides a shared wake primitive for poll loops to sleep while at capacity but wake on session completion or shutdown.
